sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Stephens Keeps an Equal Weight Rating on Global Payments Inc. (GPN)
feed.summary: Stephens has maintained an Equal Weight rating on Global Payments Inc. (GPN) but lowered its price target from $95 to $90, citing a difficult 2025 for the company and the broader payments industry. Despite this, Stephens anticipates a positive outlook for the industry in 2026. Cantor Fitzgerald also initiated coverage with a neutral rating and an $80 price target, noting that GPN's longer-term organic growth algorithm is expected to be lower due to a divestment and refresh plan.

feed.title: Globant SA stock hits 52-week low at 54.13 USD
feed.summary: Globant SA stock has fallen to a new 52-week low of $54.13, representing a 75.96% decline over the past year. Despite its profitability and solid current ratio, investor concerns persist, particularly within the tech sector. Analysts view the stock as undervalued, with price targets ranging from $68 to $150, and are closely watching strategic adjustments and the upcoming earnings report.

feed.title: Geo Group: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Geo Group Inc. (GEO) reported a fourth-quarter profit of $31.8 million, or 23 cents per share, aligning with Wall Street expectations after adjustments. The private prison operator's revenue for the quarter was $707.7 million, exceeding analyst forecasts. For the full year, Geo Group posted a profit of $254.4 million and projected future revenues between $680 million and $690 million for the current quarter.

feed.title: Gabelli Equity Trust 10% Distribution Policy Reaffirmed and Declared First Quarter Distribution of $0.15 Per Share
feed.summary: The Gabelli Equity Trust Inc. (NYSE:GAB) reaffirmed its 10% distribution policy and declared a $0.15 per share cash distribution payable on March 24, 2026. The Fund aims to pay a minimum annual distribution of 10% of its average net asset value, with the current distribution including primarily a return of capital. Shareholders are advised that the distribution rate should not be viewed as the dividend yield or total return.

feed.title: Host Hotels & Resorts stock hits 52-week high at $20.14 By Investing.com

feed.title: TD Cowen Raises Becton, Dickinson and Company (NYSE:BDX) Price Target to $189.00

feed.title: 2 Reasons Qualcomm's Risk/Reward Is Now Red Hot
feed.summary: Qualcomm (NASDAQ: QCOM) experienced a significant downturn in early February, with shares dropping post-earnings, but is now showing signs of recovery. The article highlights two main reasons for an improved risk/reward profile: extremely oversold technicals, indicated by an RSI below 30, and increasingly bullish analyst targets, with some price targets implying over 40% upside. While acknowledging underlying concerns from the earnings report, the article suggests that the stock's recent price action and analyst sentiment present a compelling opportunity for investors.

feed.title: KeyBank scores major win with 5 family office pros
feed.summary: KeyBank has strategically expanded its services by adding a five-person family office and private capital team, led by Ward Nixon, to target ultra-wealthy families and private equity investors. This move aims to capture the growing family office market, which requires specialized banking services beyond traditional wealth management. While unlikely to dramatically alter near-term financial catalysts for KeyCorp (NYSE: KEY), this expansion is part of the bank's broader strategy to enhance its middle-market franchise and differentiate itself in a competitive market.

feed.title: Nissay implements Broadridge’s MBS solution
feed.summary: Nissay Asset Management Corporation has implemented Broadridge Financial Solutions’ mortgage-backed securities (MBS) Trade Assignment Portal (TAP). This solution automates and digitizes the assignment of trade (AOT) processes for "To Be Announced" MBS trading, allowing for electronic contract signing, mass letter sending, and real-time progress tracking. The goal is to enhance efficiency, reduce manual workload, and improve transparency for Nissay Asset Management.
